Identify the initial problem, constraints, hypotheses, experimental designs, computational analyses, observations, failures or partial results, revisions, validations, and limitations.

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Directed evolution typically uses point substitutions, but insertions and deletions (InDels) are common in natural evolution and may access unexplored sequence space. Existing InDel methods have limitations: high frameshift rates, fixed lengths, or only deletions. The goal is to develop a transposon-based method (TRIAD) to generate random in-frame InDel libraries and explore their functional effects on phosphotriesterase (wtPTE), comparing to point mutations.

section_gists.heading: Accessing unexplored regions of sequence space in directed enzyme evolution via insertion/deletion mutagenesis
section_gists.gist: Introduces TRIAD, a transposon-based method for random in-frame InDels (±3,6,9 bp). Validates by evolving arylesterase activity in phosphotriesterase. Finds InDel variants show more compromised average activity but higher proportion of adapted variants, with distinct trade-offs and improvements in kcat rather than KM, suggesting access to novel adaptive solutions.
process_signals: InDels are overlooked in lab evolution despite natural prevalence.
Existing InDel methods have high frameshift rates or limited diversity.
TRIAD designed to avoid frameshifts and generate diverse in-frame InDels.
Hypothesis: InDels can access functional innovations not reachable by point mutations.
methods: Transposon-based mutagenesis (TRIAD) for random in-frame InDels
metrics: Trade-off between phosphotriesterase and arylesterase activities
failures_or_limits: Average activity of TRIAD variants is more compromised than point mutants.
Trade-off can be severe (20-35 fold increase in arylesterase with 60-400 fold decrease in phosphotriesterase).

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist: Describes TRIAD method: transposon insertion, cassette ligation, and digestion to create deletions (-3,-6,-9 bp) and insertions (+3,+6,+9 bp). Validates library quality by Sanger and NGS, showing single modifications, no incidental mutations, and good coverage. Notes theoretical diversity limited by sequence context and transformation efficiency. Screens libraries for arylesterase activity, identifying hits with improved activity and distinct trade-offs compared to point mutants.
process_signals: TRIAD uses engineered transposons TransDel and TransIns.
Deletion libraries: MlyI digestion and self-ligation; longer deletions use Del2/Del3 cassettes.
Insertion libraries: asymmetric TransIns with NotI/MlyI ends; Ins1/Ins2/Ins3 cassettes with randomized triplets.
Library quality: Sanger sequencing shows single modifications, no incidental mutations; NGS confirms diversity and distribution.
Theoretical diversity high but practical size limited by transformation efficiency (>10^6 CFU).
Screening reveals InDel variants with improved arylesterase activity, often with strong trade-off against native phosphotriesterase activity.
Adaptive InDels cluster in loops 2,7,8.
methods: Type IIS restriction enzyme cloning (MlyI, AcuI)
Cassette ligation (Del2, Del3, Ins1, Ins2, Ins3)
Next-generation sequencing for library quality assessment
failures_or_limits: Theoretical diversity not fully sampled for +6 and +9 bp insertions due to transformation limits.
Some InDels introduce stop codons or redundancy.
Most InDel variants show reduced native activity.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Contextualizes InDels in natural evolution: they are common but under strong purifying selection, often short and in loops. InDels can cause larger structural changes than substitutions, potentially leading to functional innovation. TRIAD provides experimental access to InDel libraries, enabling analysis of their contributions. Notes that InDels are more deleterious on average, but can drive functional divergence. Emphasizes need for reliable randomization methods.
process_signals: InDels are frequent in nature but subject to stronger purifying selection than substitutions.
Short InDels (1-5 residues) occur mostly in loops.
InDels alter backbone length, causing global structural effects, unlike local side-chain changes of substitutions.
InDels can reposition active site residues, change specificity, or affect oligomerization.
Experimental evidence shows InDels are more deleterious on average (corroborated by this work and TEM-1 study).
InDels contribute to functional divergence in several enzyme families.
TRIAD enables systematic study of InDel effects in directed evolution.
failures_or_limits: InDels are generally more deleterious than point mutations.
Methods to introduce InDels were previously lacking, hindering understanding.

section_gists.heading: Methods
section_gists.gist: Details experimental procedures: plasmid and transposon construction, removal of internal restriction sites, transposon insertion library generation, deletion and insertion library construction via cassette ligation and digestion, and transformation. Notes use of TriNEx library for comparison with point substitutions.
process_signals: Recognition sites for MlyI, NotI, AcuI must be removed from target and vector.
Transposon insertion libraries generated by in vitro transposition, transformation, and selection.
Deletion libraries: MlyI digestion, self-ligation; for -6/-9, insert Del2/Del3 cassettes, then MlyI removal and self-ligation.
Insertion libraries: NotI/MlyI digestion, ligation of Ins cassettes, AcuI digestion to remove shuttle, self-ligation.
TriNEx library (point substitutions) used for comparison.
Libraries stored as plasmid solutions.
methods: Cassette construction (Del2, Del3, Ins1, Ins2, Ins3)
In vitro transposition with MuA transposase
failures_or_limits: Requires removal of internal restriction sites beforehand.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: InDels are an underexplored source of functional diversity in directed evolution.
TRIAD can generate high-quality in-frame InDel libraries without incidental mutations.
InDel variants will show distinct functional profiles compared to point mutants, with more severe trade-offs but potential for novel adaptations.
InDels may enable access to fitness landscape regions not reachable by substitutions alone.
uncertainties_to_resolve: How does the functional effect of InDels compare quantitatively to point mutations across different enzymes?
Can TRIAD be generalized to other proteins and selection schemes?
What are the structural mechanisms underlying the adaptive InDels?
How does the trade-off severity relate to the position and length of InDels?
Can iterative rounds of TRIAD evolution yield further improvements?

source.title: Accessing unexplored regions of sequence space in directed enzyme evolution via insertion/deletion mutagenesis

initial_request.research_problem: Directed evolution typically uses point substitutions, but insertions and deletions (InDels) are common in natural evolution and may access unexplored sequence space. Existing InDel methods have limitations: high frameshift rates, fixed lengths, or only deletions. The goal is to develop a transposon-based method (TRIAD) to generate random in-frame InDel libraries and explore their functional effects on phosphotriesterase (wtPTE), comparing to point mutations.
initial_request.known_context: InDels are frequent and beneficial in natural protein evolution but overlooked in laboratory evolution.
quantifiable_goals: Compare fitness effects of InDels vs. point substitutions
Identify InDel variants with improved arylesterase activity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Directed evolution relies on point substitutions; InDels are underexplored.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: InDels may access functional sequence space not reachable by substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: InDel mutagenesis can yield functional variants and explore new evolutionary trajectories.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop a transposon-based method to generate random in-frame InDel libraries.
evolution_trajectory.observation: InDels remain an overlooked source of variation despite their frequent and functionally beneficial occurrence in natural protein evolution.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design TRIAD deletion and insertion cassettes.

evidence.quote_or_span: insertions or deletions (InDels) remain an overlooked source of variation despite their frequent and functionally beneficial occurrence in natural protein evolution

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No method for random in-frame InDels of multiple lengths.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate deletions of 3, 6, 9 bp and insertions of +3, +6, +9 bp.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Transposon-based cassettes with restriction sites can create precise in-frame InDels.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design TransDel for deletions and TransIns for insertions.

parameters.deletion_cassette: TransDel with MlyI sites, CamR marker; Del2/Del3 with KanR
parameters.insertion_cassette: TransIns with NotI/MlyI ends; Ins1/Ins2/Ins3 with randomized triplets and AcuI sites

evolution_trajectory.observation: TransDel enables removal of 3 bp; Del2/Del3 enable 6 and 9 bp deletions. TransIns enables +3, +6, +9 bp insertions.

evidence.quote_or_span: TransDel carries a selection marker (resistance gene against chloramphenicol; CamR) enabling the recovery of in vitro transposition products after transformation into E. coli.
Mlyl digestion removes TransDel together with 8 bp of the target DNA (4 bp at each end), leaving blunt ends and resulting in the removal of a contiguous 3 bp sequence from the target DNA
blunt-ended cassettes Del2 or Del3 are ligated into the gap left upon TransDel removal for the generation of 6 and 9 bp deletions, respectively.
TransDel is an asymmetric transposon with Mlyl at one end and Notl at the other end.
cloni® 10G cells subsequently plated on LB-agar-amp, yielding libraries of wtPTE variants with +3, +6 or +9 bp random insertions.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to validate library generation and quality.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: TRIAD can produce diverse in-frame InDel libraries in wtPTE.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate six libraries (-3, -6, -9, +3, +6, +9 bp) and validate by Sanger and deep sequencing.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Libraries generated; Sanger sequencing of 121 variants confirmed single modifications without incidental mutations. Deep sequencing showed >85% position coverage and high diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: validated by generating libraries of InDel variants of Brevundimonas diminuta phosphotriesterase (wtPTE)
All 121 Sanger-sequenced variants displayed only a single modification from the initial transposon insertion, without any incidental mutations
Good coverage of possible positions in the insertion libraries translates into high diversity at most positions in wtPTE
10 or more distinct DNA insertions were observed between 66% (+3 bp) and 80% (+6 and +9 bp libraries) of positions

To demonstrate that TRIAD libraries allow access to functional innovation via adaptive InDels, all the libraries generated from the full-length wtPTE gene (six libraries in total: -3, -6, -9, +3, +6 and +9 bp) were subjected to two parallel screening campaigns to identify variants with enhanced arylesterase activity against either 4-nitrophenyl butyrate (4-NPB) or 2-naphthyl hexanoate (2-NH) (Fig.
Six independent libraries of wtPTE InDel variants were generated, comprising three deletion (-3, -6 and -9 bp) and three insertion libraries (+3, +6 and +9 bp).

evolution_trajectory.observation: 39% of InDels had adjacent amino acid substitutions. +6 and +9 bp libraries undersampled (24% and 2% protein diversity captured).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to functional comparison despite undersampling.
